chapters.title: Greetings & Basic Phrases
chapters.description: Learn essential Japanese greetings and polite expressions.

lessons.prompt: What does 'おはようございます (Ohayou gozaimasu)' mean?
lessons.explanation: 'Ohayou gozaimasu' is the formal good morning. Casual: 'Ohayou'. Use formal with strangers and superiors.

lessons.prompt: はじめ___。(Nice to meet you.)
lessons.explanation: 'Hajimemashite' = nice to meet you. Said when meeting someone for the first time. Followed by 'yoroshiku onegaishimasu'.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'Thank you very much'
lessons.correctAnswer: ありがとうございます
lessons.hint: 'Arigatou gozaimasu' is the formal thank you. Casual: 'arigatou'.

lessons.prompt: When do you say 'いただきます (Itadakimasu)'?
lessons.explanation: 'Itadakimasu' is said before eating — expressing gratitude for the food. 'Gochisousama deshita' after eating.

lessons.prompt: すみ___。(Excuse me / I'm sorry.)
lessons.explanation: 'Sumimasen' = excuse me / I'm sorry (mild). Also used to get someone's attention. 'Gomen nasai' = deeper apology.

chapters.title: Hiragana Foundations
chapters.description: Learn to read and write basic hiragana characters.

lessons.prompt: What sound does 'あ' make?
lessons.explanation: The five vowels in Japanese: あ(a), い(i), う(u), え(e), お(o). These are the foundation of hiragana.

lessons.prompt: The hiragana for 'KA' is ___.
lessons.explanation: か = ka. The K-row: か(ka), き(ki), く(ku), け(ke), こ(ko).

lessons.prompt: What word do these hiragana spell? さ・く・ら
lessons.hint: さ=sa, く=ku, ら=ra. What beautiful Japanese word does this spell?

lessons.prompt: What does 'な・ま・え' (namae) mean?
lessons.explanation: 'Namae' (名前) = name. 'Watashi no namae wa...' = My name is...

lessons.prompt: にほん = Ni___n (Japan)
lessons.explanation: 'Nihon' (にほん) = Japan. ほ = ho. Japan's name means 'origin of the sun'.

chapters.title: Numbers & Counting
chapters.description: Count in Japanese and use numbers in everyday situations.

lessons.prompt: What is 'ご (go)' in English?
私は___歳です。(I am twenty years old.) ___=にじゅう
lessons.explanation: 'Nijuu' (二十) = 20. 'Ni' (2) + 'juu' (10) = 20. Japanese uses this additive system for numbers.

lessons.prompt: How do you say 'three people' using the counter for people?
lessons.hint: 'Nin' (にん) is the counter for people. San (3) + nin = sannin.

lessons.prompt: What counter is used for flat objects like paper and tickets?
lessons.explanation: 'Mai' is for flat/thin objects: paper, tickets, stamps, plates. '一枚' (ichimai) = one sheet.

lessons.prompt: コーヒーを___ください。(Please give me one coffee.) ___=いっぱい
lessons.explanation: 'Ippai' (一杯) = one cup/glass. 'Hai' (杯) is the counter for drinks. 'Ichi' + 'hai' = 'ippai' (sound change).

chapters.title: Basic Sentence Patterns
chapters.description: Build simple Japanese sentences using は, が, を, and です.

lessons.prompt: What is the function of 'は (wa)' in a Japanese sentence?
lessons.explanation: 'Wa' (は) marks the topic of the sentence. 'Watashi wa Tarō desu' = As for me, I am Taro.

lessons.prompt: これ___ 本です。(This is a book.)
lessons.explanation: 'Kore wa hon desu' = This (topic) is a book. 'Kore' = this, 'hon' = book, 'desu' = is/am/are.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'I eat sushi'
lessons.hint: 'Wo' (を) marks the direct object. 'Tabemasu' = eat (polite form).

lessons.prompt: What does 'じゃありません' (ja arimasen) mean after a noun?
lessons.explanation: 'Desu' = is. 'Ja arimasen' (or 'de wa arimasen') = is not. 'Gakusei ja arimasen' = is not a student.

lessons.prompt: どこ___ トイレですか。(Where is the toilet?)
lessons.explanation: 'Doko ni ~ ga arimasu ka' or 'doko desu ka' for locations. 'Toire wa doko desu ka' = Where is the toilet?

chapters.title: Shopping & Prices
chapters.description: Shop in Japan, ask prices, and make transactions confidently.

lessons.prompt: How do you ask 'How much is this?' in Japanese?
lessons.explanation: 'Ikura desu ka' = How much is it? 'Kore wa ikura desu ka' = How much is this?

lessons.prompt: ___をください。(Please give me this.)
lessons.explanation: 'Kore o kudasai' = Please give me this. 'Kudasai' = please give (me). Essential shopping phrase.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'Do you have a smaller size?'
lessons.correctAnswer: もっと小さいサイズはありますか
lessons.hint: 'Motto' = more. 'Chiisai' = small. 'Saizu' = size. 'Arimasu ka' = do you have?

lessons.prompt: What does 'レジ袋はいりますか' (reji bukuro wa irimasu ka) mean?
options: Do you need a plastic bag?
lessons.correctAnswer: Do you need a plastic bag?
lessons.explanation: 'Reji bukuro' = register bag/plastic bag. 'Irimasu ka' = do you need? You'll hear this at every Japanese shop.

lessons.prompt: カードで___できますか。(Can I pay by card?)
lessons.explanation: 'Kaado de shiharai dekimasu ka' = Can I pay by card? 'Shiharai' = payment. 'De' = by means of.
